creator: dave@daveg.ca
text:
Created attachment 253266
plasma clock display.

I'm not sure where in plasma this belongs, so I'm starting with submitting the desktop.  I have a FreeBSD desktop and my system timezone is EST5EDT (New York or Toronto)  If I ask it:

[1:1:301]dgilbert@strike:~> date
Mon Sep  2 00:11:37 EDT 2024
[1:2:302]dgilbert@strike:~> unset TZ
[1:3:303]dgilbert@strike:~> date
Mon Sep  2 04:11:41 UTC 2024

But my plasma desktop says: (see image attachment)

Now... you'll note I've configured the clock to display the timezone it's using.  UTC-4 is correct ... but the time is not (is within minutes of the above).

So... to duplicate:

- have a system with UTC system clock
- have a system wide default timezone that is not UTC (I assume)
- run kde plasma desktop.
